description: Webhook events emitted by a GitHub App or GitHub-webhook-producing integration. Refs are dot-paths evaluated against the payload; numeric segments index arrays (pull_requests.0.number) and `||` expresses first-non-empty fallback chains. resource_key_template is substituted from refs after extraction — keep the shape consistent across events that should thread to the same conversation.

issues.opened.description: A new issue is created in a repository. Fires once per issue open, never for issues converted from PRs or from other tools.

issues.closed.description: An issue is closed. issue_state_reason tells you why: completed (default), not_planned, reopened, duplicate, or null.

issues.edited.description: Title or body of an issue was changed. The `changes` object in the payload holds the prior values.

issues.labeled.description: A label was added to an issue. The specific label is at payload.label (use label_name to filter to a specific label via conditions).

issues.unlabeled.description: A label was removed from an issue.

issues.assigned.description: An assignee was added to an issue. `assignee.login` is the single new assignee for this event; `issue.assignees` holds the cumulative list.

issues.unassigned.description: An assignee was removed from an issue.

issues.milestoned.description: An issue was added to a milestone.

issue_comment.created.description: A comment was added to an issue or pull request conversation. GitHub does NOT emit a separate pull_request_comment event for PR conversation comments — they arrive as issue_comment.created with issue.pull_request populated. pr_url is set only for PR comments and empty for pure issue comments; use it to scope a trigger to one side (path=pr_url operator=exists or not_exists).

issue_comment.edited.description: A comment on an issue or PR conversation was edited.

issue_comment.deleted.description: A comment on an issue or PR conversation was deleted.

pull_request.opened.description: A new PR is opened. Fires for both drafts and non-drafts — check pr_draft to distinguish. Does NOT fire when a draft is promoted (that's ready_for_review).

pull_request.closed.description: A PR is closed or merged. pr_merged distinguishes the two: true = someone clicked Merge, false = closed without merging. merge_commit_sha is populated in both cases (GitHub computes it even on close), but merged_at / merged_by are only set when actually merged.

pull_request.edited.description: PR title or body was changed. Noisy — most bots fire on every save. Filter via conditions on changes.title.from / changes.body.from if you only care about specific edits.

pull_request.ready_for_review.description: A draft PR is promoted to ready-for-review. Fires exactly once per PR lifetime unless converted back to draft and re-promoted.

pull_request.converted_to_draft.description: A non-draft PR is demoted back to draft. Useful signal to pause agent work on a PR.

pull_request.synchronize.description: New commits were pushed to the PR's head branch. payload.before and payload.after hold the SHAs. Fires on every push, so can be noisy — consider conditions on sender.type != 'Bot' if your bots push to their own PRs.

pull_request.review_requested.description: A reviewer (user or team) was requested on a PR. Exactly one of requested_reviewer_login / requested_team_slug is populated per event.

pull_request.review_request_removed.description: A previously-requested reviewer was removed from a PR.

pull_request.labeled.description: A label was added to a PR. Typical uses: 'needs-revision', 'ready-to-merge', 'wip' as workflow signals.

pull_request.unlabeled.description: A label was removed from a PR.

pull_request.assigned.description: A PR was assigned to someone.

pull_request.auto_merge_enabled.description: Auto-merge was turned on for a PR.

pull_request.auto_merge_disabled.description: Auto-merge was turned off for a PR.

pull_request_review.submitted.description: A reviewer submitted a review on a PR. review_state is one of: approved, changes_requested, commented. Fires ONCE per review even if the review contained many inline comments.

pull_request_review.dismissed.description: A review was dismissed (typically because new commits invalidated it).

pull_request_review.edited.description: A submitted review's body was edited.

pull_request_review_comment.created.description: A reviewer left an inline comment on a specific diff line. Each line comment of a submitted review fires this AND is included in the subsequent review payload — if you subscribe to both, you'll see duplicates. Standalone 'single comments' (submitted without a review) fire this event without an accompanying pull_request_review event.

pull_request_review_comment.edited.description: A review comment on a diff line was edited.

check_suite.completed.description: The best CI-done signal. Fires ONCE per commit when every check_run in the suite has finished (Actions, third-party apps, everything). Prefer this over check_run.completed for PR-level CI reactions — drops per-push event volume from ~N to 1. pull_requests[] is empty for PRs from forks and for commits not in any PR (direct push to main, etc.); use head_sha + head_branch as fallback keys.

check_suite.requested.description: A check suite was created on a commit, typically right after a push. Fires before any check_run inside it has started. Useful for a 'CI starting' signal.

check_suite.rerequested.description: A user clicked 'Re-run all checks' on a check suite.

check_run.completed.description: One individual check (Actions job, third-party-app check, etc.) finished. High-volume — for PR-level reactions prefer check_suite.completed. check_run.conclusion is one of: success, failure, neutral, cancelled, timed_out, action_required, stale, skipped. pull_requests[] has the same fork/cross-repo gaps as check_suite.

check_run.created.description: A check run started on a commit.

check_run.rerequested.description: A user clicked 'Re-run' on a single check.

check_run.requested_action.description: A user clicked an action button inside a check run's output (e.g. 'fix this lint'). `requested_action.identifier` is the action key the button declared.

workflow_run.completed.description: A GitHub Actions workflow finished. Subset of check_suite.completed (Actions only, not third-party apps). workflow_run.event tells you what triggered the workflow (push, pull_request, workflow_dispatch, etc.). Check workflow_run.conclusion: success, failure, cancelled, timed_out, action_required, stale, neutral, skipped.

workflow_run.requested.description: A workflow run was queued to start.

workflow_job.completed.description: A single job inside a workflow run finished. One per job — fires multiple times per workflow_run. Use workflow_job.conclusion (same enum as workflow_run.conclusion).

workflow_job.queued.description: A workflow job was queued and is waiting for a runner.

workflow_job.waiting.description: A workflow job is waiting for approval (manual approval for environments, required reviewers, etc.).

deployment_status.created.description: A deployment's status changed. deployment_status.state: error, failure, inactive, in_progress, queued, pending, success. workflow_run/workflow fields are populated when the deployment originated from an Actions workflow.

push.description: Commits (or tags) were pushed to a ref. payload.ref is the full ref (refs/heads/main, refs/tags/v1.0). Use `created`/`deleted`/`forced` to distinguish branch/tag lifecycle actions. Push events where the ref is a tag typically have commits=[] and head_commit=null.

create.description: A branch or tag was created. payload.ref holds the short name (e.g. `main` or `v1.0`), NOT a `refs/heads/…` path — this differs from push. Use ref_type to distinguish branch vs tag. Not fired for fork creation; GitHub's docs list legacy exclusions (creation of repositories and changes to files).

delete.description: A branch or tag was deleted. payload.ref holds the short name (same convention as create).

release.published.description: A release became publicly visible (non-draft). Use release.prerelease to distinguish stable from pre-release.

release.released.description: A release marked as latest was published. Fires in addition to release.published when the release is the latest non-prerelease.

release.created.description: A draft release was created. Fires before it becomes public. release.draft is true.

release.edited.description: An existing release's metadata (name, body, tag, etc.) was edited.

discussion.created.description: A new discussion thread was opened.

discussion.answered.description: A comment on a Q&A-category discussion was marked as the answer. `answer` holds the comment chosen.

discussion.edited.description: A discussion's title or body was edited.

discussion.labeled.description: A label was added to a discussion.

discussion_comment.created.description: A new comment (or reply) was posted on a discussion thread. comment_parent_id is set on replies.

commit_comment.created.description: A comment was posted on a commit (outside any PR context). Rare — most commit-level discussion happens via PR review comments.

fork.description: Someone created a fork of the repository.

star.created.description: Someone starred the repository. Pair with star.deleted if tracking churn.

watch.started.description: Legacy event; GitHub fires this for 'star' actions. Roughly equivalent to star.created. Prefer star.created for new triggers.

action.description: opened, edited, deleted, pinned, unpinned, closed, reopened, assigned, unassigned, labeled, unlabeled, locked, unlocked, transferred, milestoned, or demilestoned

issue.description: The issue: id, number, title, body, state, state_reason, user, labels[], assignees[], milestone, created_at, updated_at, closed_at, html_url, author_association, reactions, locked, active_lock_reason

changes.description: Prior values for edited actions: changes.title.from, changes.body.from

assignee.description: The user added/removed for assigned/unassigned actions

label.description: The label added/removed for labeled/unlabeled: id, name, color, description, default

milestone.description: The milestone for milestoned/demilestoned: id, number, title, state, due_on

installation.description: Present when delivered via a GitHub App installation

issue.description: The issue OR pull request. issue.pull_request.url is populated when the comment is on a PR — use it as the issue-vs-PR discriminator.

comment.description: id, body, user, created_at, updated_at, html_url, author_association, reactions, performed_via_github_app

action.description: opened, edited, closed, reopened, assigned, unassigned, review_requested, review_request_removed, labeled, unlabeled, synchronize, converted_to_draft, ready_for_review, locked, unlocked, auto_merge_enabled, auto_merge_disabled, enqueued, dequeued, milestoned, demilestoned

pull_request.description: id, number, title, body, state, draft, merged, merged_at, merged_by, merge_commit_sha, user, head (ref/sha/repo), base (ref/sha/repo), labels[], assignees[], requested_reviewers[], requested_teams[], milestone, created_at, updated_at, closed_at, html_url, diff_url, patch_url, additions, deletions, changed_files, commits, comments, review_comments, mergeable, mergeable_state, auto_merge, author_association

changes.description: Prior values for edited action: changes.title.from, changes.body.from, changes.base.ref.from, changes.base.sha.from

reason.description: Reason field on auto_merge_enabled/disabled and dequeued

review.description: id, user, body, commit_id, submitted_at, state (approved, changes_requested, commented, dismissed), html_url, author_association, pull_request_url

comment.description: id, pull_request_review_id, diff_hunk, path, position, original_position, commit_id, original_commit_id, line, original_line, start_line, start_side, side, body, user, in_reply_to_id, created_at, updated_at, html_url, author_association, reactions

check_suite.description: id, node_id, head_branch, head_sha, status (queued, in_progress, completed), conclusion (success, failure, neutral, cancelled, timed_out, action_required, stale, skipped, startup_failure), url, before, after, pull_requests[] (may be empty for fork PRs or non-PR commits), app (the GitHub App that created the suite — often null for Actions suites), created_at, updated_at, latest_check_runs_count, check_runs_url, head_commit

check_run.description: id, head_sha, node_id, external_id, name, status, conclusion, started_at, completed_at, output (title, summary, text, annotations_count, annotations_url), check_suite (nested), app, pull_requests[], html_url, details_url

workflow_run.description: id, name, display_title, node_id, head_branch, head_sha, path, run_number, event (trigger type: push, pull_request, workflow_dispatch, …), status, conclusion, workflow_id, check_suite_id, check_suite_node_id, html_url, created_at, updated_at, run_attempt, run_started_at, actor, triggering_actor, head_commit (id, message, author, committer, timestamp), head_repository, pull_requests[] (same fork/cross-repo limitations as check_suite)

workflow.description: id, node_id, name, path, state, created_at, updated_at

workflow_job.description: id, run_id, run_url, run_attempt, node_id, head_sha, head_branch, name, status, conclusion, started_at, completed_at, steps[] (each with name, status, conclusion, number, started_at, completed_at), labels[] (runner labels), runner_id, runner_name, runner_group_id, runner_group_name, workflow_name, html_url

deployment.description: Set when the job is tied to a Deployments-gated environment

deployment_status.description: id, state (error, failure, inactive, in_progress, queued, pending, success), description, environment, target_url, log_url, environment_url, created_at, updated_at, creator

deployment.description: id, sha, ref, task, environment, description, creator, created_at, updated_at, payload, original_environment, production_environment, transient_environment

workflow_run.description: Linked workflow run, if this deployment originated from Actions

after.description: SHA after the push (all zeros if deleted=true)

created.description: Whether this push created the ref

deleted.description: Whether this push deleted the ref

forced.description: Whether the push was a force push

base_ref.description: Base ref when the push merged another branch

commits.description: Commit objects pushed. Each: id, tree_id, distinct, message, timestamp, url, author, committer, added[], removed[], modified[]

head_commit.description: Most recent commit (same shape as commits[] items)

ref.description: Short name of the created ref (e.g. main, v1.0) — NOT a refs/heads/… path
Short name of the deleted ref

action.description: published, unpublished, created, edited, deleted, prereleased, released

release.description: id, tag_name, target_commitish, name, body, draft, prerelease, created_at, published_at, author, html_url, assets[], tarball_url, zipball_url, discussion_url

action.description: created, edited, deleted, pinned, unpinned, locked, unlocked, transferred, category_changed, answered, unanswered, labeled, unlabeled

discussion.description: id, number, title, body, state, category (id, name, slug, description, emoji), user, html_url, created_at, updated_at, locked, comments, answer_html_url, answer_chosen_at, answer_chosen_by, author_association

changes.description: changes.title.from / changes.body.from for edited, changes.category.from for category_changed

comment.description: id, body, user, created_at, updated_at, html_url, author_association, parent_id (for replies), reactions
id, commit_id, path, position, line, body, user, created_at, updated_at, html_url, author_association, reactions

repository.description: The source repository that was forked

starred_at.description: Timestamp when starred (null for deleted action)

action.description: Always 'started' (legacy naming; GitHub fires this for star events)
